# Governance

Version: 1.0.0

## Purpose

This document summarizes the governance model for the JLA. Governance defines who may act, what may be changed, how evidence is retained, and when escalation is required.

## Governance Rules

- Policy takes precedence over local preference
- Sensitive actions require explicit authorization and traceable evidence
- Changes to commands, pipelines, agents, and integrations must be reviewable
- Compliance checks are mandatory before release or promotion

## Control Areas

- Access control and approvals
- Audit logging and retention
- Data handling and classification
- Incident response and escalation
- Review cadence and change control
